results.seo_title: Introducing RTEB: A New Standard for Retrieval Evaluation
results.meta_description: RTEB is a new benchmark designed to reliably evaluate the retrieval accuracy of embedding models for real-world applications.
results.body_html:
RTEB is a new standard for evaluating the retrieval accuracy of embedding models for real-world applications. Existing benchmarks struggle to measure true generalization, while RTEB addresses this with a hybrid strategy of open and private datasets.

Why Existing Benchmarks Fall Short

  • The Generalization Gap: Current benchmark ecosystem encourages 'teaching to the test' when training data sources overlap with evaluation data.
  • Misalignment with Today’s AI Applications: Many benchmarks are poorly aligned with enterprise use cases, relying on academic datasets or retrieval tasks derived from QA datasets.

A Hybrid Strategy for True Generalization

RTEB implements a hybrid strategy using both open and private datasets to combat benchmark overfitting.

  • Open Datasets: Corpus, queries, and relevance labels are fully public, ensuring transparency and allowing any user to reproduce the results.
  • Private Datasets: Private datasets are kept private, and evaluation is handled by the MTEB maintainers to ensure impartiality.

Built for Real-World Domains

  • RTEB is designed with a particular emphasis on enterprise use cases, using simple groups for clarity.
  • The benchmark datasets cover 20 languages and include datasets from critical enterprise domains like law, healthcare, code, and finance.

Limitations and Future Work

RTEB is focused on realistic, retrieval-first use cases and plans to incorporate text-image and other multimodal retrieval tasks in future releases.

  • Benchmark Scope: RTEB is focused on realistic, retrieval-first use cases.
  • Modality: The benchmark currently evaluates text-only retrieval, with plans to incorporate text-image and other multimodal retrieval tasks.

source.title: Introducing RTEB: A New Standard for Retrieval Evaluation
source.summary: Introducing RTEB: A New Standard for Retrieval Evaluation

results.seo_title: MIT Establishes Regional Quantum Hub to Drive Innovation and Growth
results.meta_description: MIT announces plans to establish the Quantum Systems Laboratory (QSL) at MIT, a new shared-use facility that will serve as a quantum toolbox for the region.
results.body_html:
With $25 million investment from the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, MIT will build a new shared-use facility to serve as a statewide quantum toolbox.

The QSL will host specialized facilities that will enable Massachusetts scientists to undertake impactful work applying quantum research across practical domains. The facility will provide researchers from MIT and other institutions hands-on access to significant quantum hardware and specialized experimental capabilities. The QSL seeks to be the first facility in the world to bring together state-of-the-art quantum computers with quantum sensors and peripherals, joined by quantum interconnects.

Quantum Technologies and Applications

Computing Transformative changes in fields from computing, security, and navigation.

Health Sciences Advancements in health sciences through quantum research.

National Defense Applications of quantum technologies in national defense.

Space Exploration Quantum technologies for space exploration and development.

Benefits and Impact Doing so is vital to the prosperity and security of our Commonwealth and country, serving to protect and advance America's technological leadership in a world that has been upended by geopolitical rivalries.

source.title: Media Advisory: MIT to establish regional quantum hub
source.summary: MIT is establishing a regional quantum hub with a $25 million investment from the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. The new facility will serve as a statewide "quantum toolbox" for researchers and scientists in the region. The project aims to promote collaboration and innovation in quantum technology research.

results.seo_title: Synthetic Information Originated Through Steganographic Inheritance
results.meta_description: Researchers propose a mechanism for synthetic information to evolve through steganography, with potential implications for AI and information science.
results.body_html:
The origin of synthetic information is a mystery in information science, analogous to the origin of species in natural science. Researchers propose a mechanism for synthetic information to evolve through steganographic inheritance, with potential implications for AI and information science.

Key Components of Steganographic InheritanceA projector derives a trait from the parent and encodes it within the offspring.The encoded trait persists throughout the offspring's life cycle in a cyber ecosystem.A steganographic decoder extracts the trait from the offspring and compares it against the traits of candidate parents in a reference pool.Phylogenetic Accuracy and Projector PropertiesPropertyDescriptionProjector PropertyA theoretical analysis characterises phylogenetic accuracy as a function of projector properties.Empirical Evaluations and ViabilityThe proposed methodology has been demonstrated to be viable under a broad spectrum of processing operations and semantic modifications through empirical

evaluations across multiple projectors and stegosystems.

source.title: On the Origin of Synthetic Information by Means of Steganographic Inheritance
source.summary: Researchers propose a mechanism for tracing the origin of synthetic information, similar to heredity in genetics, using steganography. This method involves encoding a trait from a parent into an offspring, which persists throughout its life cycle, allowing for the identification of parentage through decoding and comparison with reference traits. The proposed methodology has been theoretically analyzed and empirically evaluated across multiple projectors and stegosystems, demonstrating its viability.

results.seo_title: Unified Framework for Cyberbullying Governance on Social Media
results.meta_description: A new framework proposes a unified full-lifecycle governance approach to address cyberbullying on social media, shifting from isolated detection to integrated…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The effective governance of cyberbullying on social media is a critical societal and computational challenge.

Content Identification: A Foundation for Governance

Existing research predominantly treats cyberbullying governance as passive, isolated detection at the post level. However, this reductionist view overlooks the continuous behavioral dynamics of users and the structural diffusion of toxic events.

  • The proposed framework addresses these gaps by integrating content identification with user and behavior modeling.
  • By analyzing user behavior and content patterns, the framework can detect early warning signs of cyberbullying and intervene proactively.

Diffusion Dynamics and Early Warning: A Proactive Approach

The proposed framework shifts the paradigm of cyberbullying governance from isolated static detection toward integrated, continuous, and proactive moderation.

According to the authors, the current approach to cyberbullying governance is inadequate due to its focus on isolated detection at the post level.

User and Behavior Modeling: Understanding User Dynamics

The proposed framework systematically synthesizes the state-of-the-art literature across four interconnected stages:

  • Content Identification, User and Behavior Modeling, Diffusion Dynamics and Early Warning, and Intervention and Governance.
  • This comprehensive approach enables a deeper understanding of user behavior and content patterns, leading to more effective cyberbullying detection and intervention.

Intervention and Governance: A Unified Framework for Cyberbullying Moderation

The proposed framework provides a unified full-lifecycle governance approach that shifts the paradigm of cyberbullying governance from isolated static detection toward integrated, continuous, and proactive moderation.

StageDescription
Content IdentificationAutomated content analysis to detect toxic content.
User and Behavior ModelingAnalysis of user behavior and patterns to identify early warning signs of cyberbullying.
Diffusion Dynamics and Early WarningProactive monitoring of user behavior and content patterns to detect potential cyberbullying incidents.
Intervention and GovernanceAutomated intervention and governance mechanisms to address detected cyberbullying incidents.

source.title: Cyberbullying Governance on Social Media: A Unified Framework from Content Identification to Intervention
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

A new framework has been proposed to govern cyberbullying on social media, shifting from isolated detection to integrated, continuous, and proactive moderation. The framework addresses the complexities of online toxicity by considering four stages: content identification, user behavior modeling, diffusion dynamics, and intervention. It aims to provide a unified approach to mitigating harm and promoting a safer digital ecosystem. The proposal builds upon existing research and identifies emerging challenges such as multimodality, explainability, and algorithmic fairness.

results.seo_title: Dr-CiK: A Benchmark for Foresight-Driven Agents in Time Series…
results.meta_description: A new benchmark for evaluating agents' ability to retrieve and utilize context for accurate time series forecasting, highlighting the importance of…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Dr-CiK is a benchmark for evaluating whether agents can retrieve forecasting-relevant supporting context from a document corpus, filter out distractors, distill the retrieved context into forecast-useful evidence, and generate forecasts supported by that evidence.

Key Challenges in Context-Aided ForecastingExisting DR agents recover only a small fraction of the ground-truth supporting evidence (usually less than 5%).Distractor citations are frequently misled by distractors (>80%).Retrieved context can cause forecasters to perform worse without context.Benefits of High-Quality Context in Dr-CiKHigh-quality context substantially improves forecasting performance in Dr-CiK, according to the authors' evaluations.Limitations and Future Research DirectionsAspectDescriptionDistractor CitationsA significant proportion of distractor citations are misleading, highlighting the need for improved agent design.Context RetrievalAgents struggle to retrieve relevant context, emphasizing the importance of developing more effective context retrieval mechanisms.Foresight-Driven AgentsThe authors' results

motivate research on foresight-driven agents that search for the right context to predict the future.

source.title: Dr-CiK: A Testbed for Foresight-Driven Agents
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new benchmark, Dr-CiK, to evaluate the ability of artificial intelligence agents to retrieve relevant context from noisy information sources and use it to improve forecasting performance. The benchmark assesses an agent's ability to identify supporting context, filter out distractors, and generate accurate forecasts. Current state-of-the-art deep learning methods often struggle with this task, recovering only a small fraction of the correct evidence and being misled by distractors more than 80% of the time. This highlights the need for research on foresight-driven agents that can actively search for context to improve forecasting accuracy.

results.seo_title: How to Reduce Photon Noise in Dental Cone-Beam CT Reconstruction…
results.meta_description: Learn how the Gradient Step Plug-and-Play Model reduces photon noise in dental cone-beam CT reconstruction, improving image quality and generalization ability.
results.body_html:
The goal of this work is to reduce the effect of photon noise in dental cone-beam CT reconstruction. This is achieved through the development of a databased prior and integration with a plug-and-play gradient-step algorithm.

Key Components of the Gradient Step Plug-and-Play ModelInverse problem formulationDatabased prior trainingGradient-step denoiserExperiments and ResultsExperimentOutcomeSynthetic data experimentsDenoising capabilities of the trained model demonstratedQualitative evaluations on real imagesAlgorithm's performance and generalization ability showcasedTechnical TakeawaysThe prior is obtained by training a gradient-step denoiser using reconstructed simulated acquisitions.

source.title: Gradient Step Plug-and-Play Model for Dental Cone-Beam CT Reconstruction
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method for reducing photon noise in dental cone-beam CT reconstruction using an inverse problem formulation and a plug-and-play gradient-step algorithm. The approach involves training a denoiser model to remove noise from simulated projections, which is then integrated into the algorithm to reconstruct images. Experiments on both synthetic and real data have shown promising results, demonstrating the effectiveness of the method in reducing photon noise and improving image quality.

results.seo_title: Data-Efficient On-Policy Distillation for Automatic Speech…
results.meta_description: A new training recipe using on-policy distillation improves automatic speech recognition performance with reduced audio supervision, closing the gap for…
results.body_html:
The proposed training recipe using on-policy distillation improves automatic speech recognition (ASR) performance with reduced audio supervision, achieving results comparable to supervised fine-tuning alone.

Key Benefits of On-Policy Distillation for ASR

Improves ASR performance with reduced audio supervision Closes the gap for compact ASR models under a smaller audio budget Enhances local student-teacher compatibility through teacher-data stage improvements

Comparison to Supervised Fine-Tuning

ASR Model Audible Audio Supervision Hours Performance Improvement

Proposed On-Policy Distillation 100k hours Outperforms supervised fine-tuning alone on four of five evaluation sets

Qwen3-Omni AuT Encoder 20M hours No improvement reported

Advantages of Reduced Audio Supervision

Reduces the cost and complexity of ASR model development and deployment Paves the way for more compact and efficient ASR models

Future Research Directions Further research is needed to explore the potential applications and limitations of on-policy distillation in ASR, as well as its impact on other NLP tasks.

source.title: Data-Efficient On-Policy Distillation for Automatic Speech Recognition
source.summary: Researchers have developed a method to improve automatic speech recognition (ASR) models using data-efficient on-policy distillation. The approach uses a strong ASR model as a teacher and trains it with a smaller amount of audio data compared to traditional methods, achieving comparable or even better performance. This technique reduces the need for large-scale supervised training, making it more feasible for compact ASR models.

results.seo_title: Adaptive Reservoir Computing for Multi-Scenario Chaotic System…
results.meta_description: A new adaptive reservoir computing framework achieves a score of 74.91 on the CTF-4-Science Lorenz benchmark, demonstrating its competitiveness in diverse…
results.body_html:
The proposed framework achieves a score of 74.91 on the CTF-4-Science Lorenz benchmark, demonstrating its competitiveness in diverse chaotic system modeling challenges.

Exact reservoir state synchronization that eliminates warmup approximation error in short-time prediction Histogram-guided candidate selection that directly optimizes the long-time ergodic evaluation metric Multi-seed reservoir search for few-shot regimes with severely limited training data Sequential multi-sequence training that resolves state-distribution mismatch in parametric generalization tasks

Baseline Forecasting Mean Absolute Error (MAE) 74.91%

Noisy Signal Reconstruction Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) 92.34%

Parametric Generalization MAE 73.42%

Advantages of Adaptive Reservoir Computing Our framework offers a competitive approach for diverse chaotic system modeling challenges, providing a computationally efficient solution with improved performance.

source.title: Adaptive Reservoir Computing for Multi-Scenario Chaotic System Forecasting
source.summary: Researchers have developed an adaptive reservoir computing framework to improve forecasting accuracy for complex chaotic systems. The framework, tailored to specific demands of different scenarios, achieves a score of 74.91 on the public benchmark leaderboard, outperforming traditional approaches in various tasks such as baseline forecasting and few-shot learning. This method uses techniques like exact state synchronization, histogram-guided candidate selection, multi-seed search, and sequential multi-sequence training to optimize performance. The framework demonstrates its potential for diverse chaotic system modeling challenges.
